title: WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM - 211info
text: JOSEPHINE COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH GRANTS PASS WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF WIC is a public health program designed to improve health outcomes and influence lifetime nutrition and health behaviors for women, infants, and children. Nutrition education is the cornerstone of the WIC program; other services include breastfeeding support, monthly benefits for supplemental nutritious foods, and referrals to community resources. 715 NW Dimmick Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Applicants must meet four criteria to be eligible for WIC: -Live in Oregon (US citizenship is NOT required to participate in WIC) -Be a pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding woman, or an infant or child under age 5. Fathers, foster parents, and other guardians can also apply on behalf of a qualifying child. -Have a household income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Families receiving OHP, SNAP, or TANF are automatically income-eligible for WIC. -Have a nutritional risk (assessed at time of appointment) Hours: Varies by location Email (541) 474-5333 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 474-5333 Text Short Code 85511 Text Jocwic Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information and to schedule an appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of ID for parent/guardian and child, proof of address, and proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Curry and Josephine Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5:45pm, Friday 8am-noon Closed 8am-noon on the first Tuesday of each month TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on the corner of NW Dimmick and NW A Street Services: WIC Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SCREENING/DIAGNOSTIC SERVICES - SCREENWISE-ENROLLING HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE HUMAN REPRODUCTION - OREGON REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH PROGRAM CLINIC INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! PUBLIC HEALTH - COMMUNICABLE DISEASE PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - TUBERCULOSIS PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES AND IMMUNIZATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
